name: The Beatles
description: The Fab Four — members, songs, albums, and history

questions.q: In which English port city were The Beatles formed?

source.notes: Formed in Liverpool in 1960.

questions.q: How many members made up The Beatles during their recording career?

source.notes: John, Paul, George, and Ringo.

questions.q: Which 1969 Beatles album features the band walking across a zebra crossing on its cover?

source.notes: Photographed August 8, 1969 outside the EMI studios in St John's Wood, London.

questions.q: John, Paul, George and who was the fourth Beatle?

source.notes: Ringo Starr replaced Pete Best on drums in 1962.

questions.q: What color was the submarine in The Beatles' 1966 song and 1968 animated film?

source.notes: 'Yellow Submarine' was released as a single in August 1966.

questions.q: In which city was John Lennon shot and killed in December 1980?

source.notes: Shot outside The Dakota apartment building on the Upper West Side.

questions.q: Which seven-minute Beatles single, released in 1968, remained their longest-running US #1?

source.notes: Spent 9 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 1968.

questions.q: What is Ringo Starr's birth name?

source.notes: Born Richard Starkey in 1940; nicknamed Ringo for the rings he wore.

questions.q: Paul McCartney goes by his middle name. What is his actual first name?

source.notes: Born James Paul McCartney on June 18, 1942.

questions.q: Which Japanese artist did John Lennon marry in 1969?

source.notes: Married in Gibraltar March 20, 1969.

questions.q: What was the name of John Lennon's first wife and the mother of his son Julian?

source.notes: Cynthia Powell; married 1962, divorced 1968.

questions.q: In what year did The Beatles officially announce their breakup?

source.notes: Paul McCartney announced his departure April 10, 1970.

questions.q: In which German city did The Beatles play residencies between 1960 and 1962 that helped hone their early sound?

source.notes: Long sets at clubs like the Indra, Kaiserkeller, Top Ten, and Star-Club.

questions.q: What is the name of the legendary Liverpool cellar club where The Beatles performed nearly 300 times before stardom?

source.notes: Located on Mathew Street, Liverpool; the band played there 1961–1963.

questions.q: Which producer, who worked with The Beatles on nearly all their albums, is commonly called the 'Fifth Beatle'?

source.notes: Signed the band to Parlophone in 1962; produced almost all their records.

questions.q: Who was the Beatles' drummer immediately before Ringo Starr joined in 1962?

source.notes: Drummed with the band 1960–1962 before being replaced.

questions.q: After The Beatles split, Paul McCartney formed which rock band with his wife Linda?

source.notes: Active 1971–1981; hits included 'Live and Let Die' and 'Band on the Run'.

questions.q: What was the name of John Lennon's pre-Beatles skiffle group, from which the band evolved?

source.notes: Named after Lennon's school, Quarry Bank High; formed 1956.

questions.q: The Beatles' 1968 self-titled double LP is almost universally known by what nickname?

source.notes: Plain white sleeve designed by Richard Hamilton; originally titled 'A Doll's House'.

questions.q: Which EMI record label did The Beatles sign with in 1962?

source.notes: Signed after being famously rejected by Decca earlier in 1962.

questions.q: In a 1966 interview, John Lennon caused an international backlash by claiming The Beatles were more popular than whom?

source.notes: Said to the London Evening Standard in March 1966; led to record burnings in the U.S.

questions.q: How old was John Lennon when he was killed by Mark David Chapman in 1980?

source.notes: Born October 9, 1940; killed December 8, 1980.
